title: narcissus pseudonarcissus proles lorifolius
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus proles lorifolius, also known as the wild daffodil, is a subspecies of the common daffodil (Narcissus pseudonarcissus) that is characterized by its drooping, bell-shaped flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal remedies, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Narcissus pseudonarcissus proles lorifolius include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and combat infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs and flowers are often dried and used in infusions, decoctions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
